title: Police Appeal for Public Assistance in Shooting Incident
content:
Hamilton Police are appealing to the public for assistance following the City’s latest shooting incident.
On Wednesday, April 8, 2026, at approximately 8:15 p.m., officers were called to a local Hospital to investigate the circumstances surrounding a 17-year-old female who had presented with a gunshot wound.
At this time, details surrounding the incident remain unclear and the parties involved are not cooperating with the investigation. Officers are continuing to gather information to determine where and how the shooting occurred.
The victim is currently recovering from her injuries.
Hamilton Police are asking anyone with information related to this incident to contact the Shooting Response Team at 905-546-4883.
To provide information anonymously, please contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit tips online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

title: Police Investigating Death on Hamilton Waterfront
content:
Hamilton Police are currently investigating after a deceased person was located in the water near Harbourside Way and Discovery Drive.
At approximately 3:15 p.m. on April 8, 2026, police were called to the waterfront after a member of the public reported what appeared to be a person in the water. Responding officers attended and confirmed the individual was deceased. Detectives, along with the Marine Unit and Forensic Services, are on scene and continuing the investigation.
The identity of the individual has not yet been confirmed. The circumstances surrounding the death remain under investigation.
A police presence will remain in the area as officers continue their work. Members of the public may notice temporary restrictions along the waterfront.
Further information will be provided as it becomes available.

title: Police Make Arrest in Downtown Stabbing Incident
content:
Hamilton Police have arrested a 28-year-old male in connection with a stabbing that occurred in lower Hamilton.
On Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at approximately 3:30 a.m., police were called to the area of King Street and Victoria Avenue North following reports of a stabbing. Initial information indicated that a male had been injured and that the suspect remained in the vicinity.
Officers arrived on scene and located a 26-year-old male suffering from a stab wound to his torso. Police immediately provided first aid before the victim was transported to hospital with serious, but non-life-threatening injuries.
The suspect was located within minutes and taken into custody without incident.
The accused has been charged with:
Assault with a Weapon
Aggravated Assault
Police continue to investigate and are asking anyone with information that may assist to contact the on-duty Division 10 Staff Sergeant at 905-546-4725.
To provide information anonymously, please contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit tips online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

title: Sudden Death Investigation
content: On April 4th, 2026, Police responded to a Sudden Death investigation at 606 Aberdeen Ave in the City of Hamilton. A 19-year-old male was located after falling through the roof of this abandoned building. He was pronounced deceased on scene, and the death is not considered suspicious.

title: Hamilton Police Conclude Investigation into Reported Sexual Assault
content:
The Hamilton Police Service Sex Crimes Unit has concluded its investigation into a reported abduction and sexual assault in the Hamilton East Mountain area during the late evening hours of March 28, 2026.
Through the course of the investigation, all involved parties have been identified. Investigators have determined that there is no ongoing threat to public safety in relation to this incident.
Hamilton Police recognize the impact that reports of this nature have on the community. We remain committed to thoroughly investigating all reports of sexual violence and supporting individuals who come forward.
There is no guaranteed way to prevent sexual assault. Sharing information about these incidents is intended to keep the community informed. Individuals are encouraged to trust their instincts. For confidential support, please contact the Sexual Assault Centre’s 24-hour support line at 905-525-4162 or visit
www.sacha.ca
, the Sexual Assault/Domestic Violence Care Centre at
www.hhsc.ca/sadv
, or the Centre de Santé Communautaire at
www.cschn.ca
.
To provide information anonymously, call Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit tips online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

title: Hamilton Police Investigating Abduction/ Sexual Assault
content:
Hamilton Police is presently investigating an abduction and sexual assault which occurred on the Hamilton East Mountain area during the late evening hours of March 28, 2026.  The adult female victim was walking home when she was forced into a motor vehicle and driven to a separate location where she was sexually assaulted.  This appears to be a targeted incident.  The victim has been located, and has been medically assessed and released.
Police are actively looking for a lone suspect in relation to this incident.  The suspect is described as male black, late 20s to early 30s, medium build, 5’8” and was last observed wearing a black jacket.  The suspect was operating a motor vehicle but there is no description of this vehicle at the present time.
If you have any information that you believe could assist Police in this investigation, please contact Detective Paul Cottrill of the Sex Crimes Unit at 905-540-5544 or the Sex Crimes Unit non-emergency line at 905-540-5553.
We want all survivors of sexual violence to know that we believe them and we will respect their choice when and how they report the incident.
To provide information anonymously call Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
http://www.crimestoppershamilton.com
There is no guaranteed way to prevent sexual assault. Sharing the details of these assaults is intended to provide the community information, and people are encouraged to trust their instincts. For confidential support, please call the Sexual Assault Centre’s 24-hour support line at 905-525-4162 or connect at
www.sacha.ca
, or the Sexual Assault/Domestic Violence Care Centre at
www.hhsc.ca/sadv
or the Centre de Santé Communautaire
www.cschn.ca

title: Police Investigating Indecent Acts on Dundas Trails
content:
Hamilton Police are investigating two separate incidents involving indecent acts on walking trails in Dundas.
On March 21, 2026, police were called to a section of the rail trail near Lynden Road following a report of a male committing an indecent act. The victim reported that while walking along the trail, she observed a male standing approximately 100 metres away with his pants partially lowered, masturbating while staring in her direction.
On March 25, 2026, police received a second report of a similar incident on the trail. In this case, the victim observed a male nearby engaging in the same indecent act. Officers attended the area but were unable to locate the suspect.
In both incidents, the suspect is described as a white male wearing a black hoodie and white shoes.
Residents can expect to see an increased police presence in the area as the investigation continues.
Hamilton Police are reminding the public to follow these trail safety tips:
Stay on designated, well-maintained paths where possible
Avoid slopes and areas near the edge of the escarpment
Visit trails during daylight hours
Remain aware of your surroundings
Carry a fully charged cell phone
Travel in pairs or groups when possible
Anyone with information that may assist investigators is asked to contact the on-duty Division 30 Staff Sergeant at 905-546-3886.
To remain anonymous, please contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit a tip online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

title: Hamilton Police Urge Caution Following Marketplace Robberies
content:
download
Your browser does not support HTML5 video.
Buy and Sell Caution
Hamilton Police are reminding the public to exercise caution when buying and selling items through online marketplaces, following a recent robbery in the city.
On March 22, 2026, at approximately 9:30 p.m., police were called to a residence in east Hamilton for a reported robbery. A husband and wife had arranged the sale of an iPhone through Facebook Marketplace and agreed to meet the buyer at their home.
Upon arrival, the buyer was accompanied by two additional individuals. The couple observed that the group appeared uneasy. While one individual inspected the phone, the suspects suddenly fled the area on foot with the device. The husband pursued them, but the chase ended when he was sprayed with bear spray.
This incident marks the 14th marketplace-related robbery in Hamilton so far this year, with iPhones being the most commonly targeted item.
The Hamilton Police Service continues to investigate this incident and is urging residents to take precautions when conducting online transactions:
Meet in a public place:
Choose well-lit, busy locations. Hamilton Police Station 30 (400 Rymal Road East) offers a designated “Buy and Sell Zone” for safe transactions.
Check profiles carefully:
Be cautious of newly created accounts with little or no history, personal details, or transaction reviews.
Bring someone with you:
Attend meetups with a friend or inform someone of your meeting location and expected time.
Use secure payment methods:
Whenever possible, use electronic money transfers. Police have also seen incidents involving counterfeit cash.
Trust your instincts:
If something feels unsafe or suspicious, leave the situation immediately. There will always be other opportunities to buy or sell.
Anyone with information regarding this incident is asked to contact the Hamilton Police Service. Anonymous tips can be provided through Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or online at
www.crimestoppershamilton.com
.

title: Hamilton Police Warn About Fentanyl Gummies
content:
NewsRelease_SEP06_2024
Hamilton Police are issuing a warning after fentanyl-laced gummies were found during a recent warrant.
These dangerous substances, disguised as candy, pose a serious risk to public health and safety, particularly for youth who may unknowingly consume them.
What to Know:
Fentanyl is a powerful opioid, up to 100 times stronger than morphine, and even a small amount can cause a fatal overdose.
The fentanyl-laced gummies resemble regular candy, making them particularly dangerous for children and teens, as well as unsuspecting adults.
The gummies are often brightly colored and packaged in ways that could easily be mistaken for legitimate, store-bought products.
Warning Signs of Fentanyl Exposure:
Drowsiness or unresponsiveness
Difficulty breathing
Confusion or dizziness
Slow or weak pulse
If you or someone you know displays any of these symptoms after consuming a product resembling gummies or candy, call 911 immediately. Time is critical in preventing a potential overdose.
Hamilton Police urge parents, caregivers, and teachers to be vigilant and speak with children and young adults about the dangers of ingesting unknown substances.
What You Can Do:
Avoid accepting any candy or edibles from unknown sources.
Monitor what your children bring home, especially if they have been at parties or events.
Share this warning with friends and family to raise awareness.
Fentanyl is highly potent and unpredictable. Even a small amount can be deadly.
For those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website
.
We appreciate your attention to this matter and thank you for your continued support in keeping our community safe.

title: Project Odeon Dismantles Significant Illegal Opioid Producers
content:
Hamilton Police Service along with the Ontario Provincial Police, York Regional Police, Toronto Police Service and several other law enforcement agencies have dismantled a significant producer of fentanyl and other synthetic drugs in the Greater Hamilton and Toronto Area (GTHA).
Project Odeon began after police discovered a clandestine laboratory during an overdose death investigation in November 2021 at a residence on Hamilton’s Mountain. Investigators subsequently suspected a link between this death, an earlier overdose resulting in hospitalization and a death at a Toronto condominium. These all occurred within days of each other.
Opioid addiction has resulted in significant and far-reaching impacts on Hamilton. Like many other communities across the GTHA, Hamilton has been grappling with the devastating consequences of the opioid epidemic and declared a state of emergency in April 2023 related to homelessness, opioid addiction and mental health.
“As police, we have witnessed firsthand the devastating impact of opioids. These substances have not only led to a surge in addictions and overdoses but have also strained our resources as we respond to calls for service. Through Project Odeon, we have made a significant dent in the production of opioids and we believe this will have an impact on public safety in our community,” says Superintendent Marty Schulenberg.
From January 1 to July 30, 2023, there have been 606 incidents related to suspected opioid overdoses and 89-suspected drug-related deaths in Hamilton. These numbers have been steadily rising over the past five years.
“We are encouraged by the outcome of many long hours of investigatory work and thank our law enforcement partners for their continued commitment to reducing the production and distribution of drugs in Hamilton. Ultimately, the seizure of these opioids contributes to reducing the harms associated with opioid use for those who use substances, their family and friends, and the broader community,” says Julie Prieto, Director, Hamilton Public Health Services Epidemiology and Wellness Division.
By tracing the origins of some of the lab equipment, investigators were able to identify a company in the United States. This business sold three items of laboratory equipment to an Oshawa-based company. From here, investigators were able to piece together an extended drug network.
Warrants were executed in Hamilton, Mount Albert, Smithville, Toronto, Whitchurch-Stouffville, and Vaughan.
In total, investigators seized the following items:
An operational fentanyl drug lab at 6800 Sixteen Road, Smithville.
A dismantled fentanyl drug lab at 4057 Bethesda Road, Stouffville.
Approximately 3.5 tons of chemical byproduct from fentanyl production.
800 gallons of chemicals commonly used in the production of fentanyl
Lab equipment commonly used in the production of fentanyl
64.1 kg of illicit drugs, including 25.6 kg of fentanyl, 18 kg methamphetamine, 6 kg of ketamine
A loaded, Glock firearm and ammunition and four extended magazines
Over $350,000 of seized proceeds, including cars, jewelry, furniture and cash
ODEON Press Release Map_Version2

title: Hamilton Police Address Increase in Youth Violence
content:
Launch of Project Breathe Easy
Bear Spray - Info session
Hamilton Police Service is advising the community of a concerning increase in youth-related violent incidents, including a rise in the misuse of bear spray during altercations.
Over the past several months, officers have responded to a number of incidents involving young persons engaged in assaults, group conflicts, and weapons-related offences. Of particular concern is the growing trend of youth carrying and deploying bear spray in disputes.
Since March 3, police have responded to five incidents, with three youths facing charges.
Bear spray-related incidents have steadily increased each year between 2021 and 2025, representing a 115 per cent rise over the past five years. Youth aged 10 to 19 account for approximately 63 per cent of identified offenders. The most frequently reported offence involving bear spray is assault with a weapon.
Bear spray is designed as a wildlife deterrent and contains a highly concentrated irritant. When discharged in public spaces such as schools, malls, transit, or residential areas, it can cause significant respiratory distress, temporary blindness, panic, and serious safety risks to bystanders. When used against another person, bear spray is considered a weapon under the Criminal Code and may result in criminal charges.
While most youth in Hamilton make positive choices and contribute meaningfully to our community, the recent increase in violence highlights the need for proactive education, prevention, and enforcement.
In response to this trend, Hamilton Police have launched Project Breathe Easy, a targeted initiative aimed at reducing bear spray-related violence in the community.
Project Breathe Easy includes increased enforcement and high-visibility patrols in identified hotspot areas, proactive engagement with youth, compliance checks at retailers selling bear spray to ensure adherence to applicable legislation and responsible sales practices and ongoing monitoring and intelligence-led response to emerging concerns. The initiative combines enforcement with education to address both access and misuse.
“We don’t see a lot of bears walking around Hamilton. The reality is, we don’t have a bear problem — we have a youth safety problem that needs to be addressed,” says Chief Frank Bergen. “We’re not here to alarm families — we’re here to support them. At the same time, we are taking enforcement action where necessary to keep our community safe.”
Community Information Sessions
In addition to enforcement efforts, Hamilton Police are hosting a series of community information sessions aimed at parents and caregivers.
The sessions will focus on:
Current youth violence trends
The risks and legal implications of bear spray misuse
Warning signs parents should be aware of
How social media contributes to conflict escalation
Strategies for prevention and early intervention
Available community and mental health resources
Both in-person and virtual sessions will be offered to ensure accessibility for families across Hamilton. The virtual session will include breakout rooms in Arabic, Urdu, and French to support inclusive community dialogue.
In-Person Session
Tuesday, April 14
from 5:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. at Central Library, 55 York Blvd, Hamilton
Thursday, April 16 from 1:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m. at Valley Park Library, 970 Paramount Drive, Stoney Creek
Tuesday, April 21 from
5:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. at Les Chater Family YMCA, 356 Rymal Road, Hamilton
Virtual Session
Tuesday, May 12 at 5:30 p.m. Platform details will be provided upon registration. Register at
[email protected]
Hamilton Police emphasize that youth violence prevention requires collaboration between families, schools, community organizations, retailers, and law enforcement.
Early conversations at home, awareness of peer dynamics and online activity, and engagement in positive activities are among the strongest protective factors for young people.
“These sessions are about education, prevention, and accountability,” adds Inspector Frank Miscione. “We want parents to feel informed and supported, and we want youth to understand there are real consequences when weapons are brought into conflicts.”
Members of the community are encouraged to attend and participate in the discussions.
For more information, please visit
www.hamiltonpolice.on.ca
.
Bear Spray Poster

title: Stolen Auto, Loaded Firearm Recovered After West Mountain Arrest
content:
HPS 26659289
Hamilton Police have arrested a 28-year-old Mississauga man after officers responding to a suspicious vehicle call located a stolen vehicle and recovered a loaded firearm on Hamilton’s west mountain.
On Sunday, June 7, 2026, at approximately 3:16 a.m., Hamilton Police responded to the area of Elodia Court after a witness reported a suspicious vehicle that had stopped in the neighbourhood. Officers arrived a short time later and located a grey Honda parked on Elodia Court with its lights off. The vehicle was confirmed to be a stolen auto on file with Peel Regional Police. As officers attempted to investigate, the driver rammed a police cruiser and attempted to flee. The vehicle continued along Elodia Court and struck a second police cruiser, rendering both cruisers inoperable. The driver exited the vehicle and fled on foot. Officers pursued him and arrested him a short distance away. He continued to resist police after being taken into custody and refused to identify himself.
During a search of the vehicle, officers located a loaded Glock handgun on the front passenger seat. The accused has been identified as 28-year-old Sammy Shaik of Mississauga. He has been charged with:
Possession of a Loaded Prohibited Firearm
Occupant of Motor Vehicle Knowing There was a Firearm
Dangerous Operation of a Vehicle
Possession of a Weapon Obtained by Crime
Possession of a Prohibited Weapon
Disguise with Intent
Possession of Property Obtained by Crime Over $5,000 – Stolen Auto
The investigation remains ongoing. Hamilton Police thank the witness who reported the suspicious activity. Timely information from the public can assist police in responding to incidents and advancing investigations.
Anyone with information related to this investigation is asked to contact the Division 3 Criminal Investigation Division, Detective Constable David Turnbull, at 905-546-8965.
For those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website
.

title: Police Seek Witnesses to Serious Motorcycle Collision
content:
Hamilton Police are appealing to the public for information following a serious single-vehicle motorcycle collision in the city’s east end.
On Sunday, June 7, 2026, at approximately 8:42 p.m., Hamilton Police and Hamilton Paramedic Service responded to a collision involving a motorcycle near Industrial Drive and Ottawa Street North.
A 2015 Kawasaki Ninja was travelling westbound on Nikola Tesla Boulevard, approaching a curve in the road near Industrial Drive and Ottawa Street North, when the rider was unable to navigate the curve. The motorcycle left the roadway and struck a guardrail.
The 19-year-old male rider was transported to hospital with life-altering injuries.
The Hamilton Police Collision Reconstruction Unit continues to investigate and is asking anyone who may have witnessed the collision, or who was travelling in the area at the time, to come forward. Investigators are also asking motorists to review dash camera footage and nearby residents or businesses to check surveillance video for anything that may assist the investigation.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the Collision Reconstruction Unit at
or by email at
[email protected]
.
For those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website

title: Hate-Motivated Mischief Targets Waterdown Islamic School
content:
Hamilton Police are investigating a hate-motivated mischief incident targeting a school bus owned by the Waterdown Islamic School.
Sometime between May 26 and May 31, 2026, unknown suspect(s) entered the school bus while it was parked at 499 Mohawk Road East in Hamilton. Once inside, the suspect(s) discharged a fire extinguisher throughout the vehicle, damaged seats, stole property, and left anti-Muslim graffiti.
The incident is being investigated by the Hamilton Police Hate Crime Unit.
Incidents motivated by hatred can have a significant impact on victims and create fear within the broader community. When a vehicle connected to a faith-based school is targeted, the effects are often felt beyond those directly involved. Hamilton Police take reports of hate-motivated incidents seriously and remain committed to investigating these incidents thoroughly and holding those responsible accountable.
Investigators are asking anyone who witnessed suspicious activity in the area or who may have information related to this incident to come forward.
Anyone with information is asked to contact Detective Constable Steve Mburanumwe of the Hate Crime Unit at 905-546-5511 or by email at
[email protected]
.
For those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website
.

title: Vehicle Collision Leaves Youth with Criticial Injuries
content:
Hamilton Police are investigating a serious motor vehicle collision that has left a female youth with life-threatening injuries.
On Monday, June 1, 2026, at approximately 1:45 p.m., emergency services responded to the intersection of Garth Street and Sabrina Boulevard following reports of a collision involving a pedestrian.
Preliminary investigation indicates that a female youth was struck by a southbound vehicle on Garth Street. The victim was transported to a local hospital with critical, life-threatening injuries. The driver remained at the scene and is cooperating with investigators.
Police are currently working to confirm the identity and age of the injured youth.
Investigators are asking anyone who witnessed the collision, or who may have dashcam footage from the area at the time of the incident, to contact Hamilton Police.
To provide information anonymously, contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit a tip online at crimestoppershamilton.com.

title: Missing Hamilton Man Marcin Sydor Found Deceased; Death Ruled Homicide
content:
NewsRelease_JUNE01-2026
Hamilton Police are investigating the homicide of 48-year-old Marcin SYDOR after his body was located on Friday, May 29, 2026, in the area of Pier 16 near 386 Wilcox Street.
At approximately 8:00 a.m., Hamilton Police responded after receiving a report that a body had been located along the shoreline.
Officers attended and secured the area. With the assistance of the Hamilton Police Marine Unit, the body was recovered. Hamilton Police Forensic Services attended to process the scene.
The body was transported for a post-mortem examination, which has since been completed. The deceased was positively identified as Marcin SYDOR, a 48-year-old Hamilton man who had been reported missing to Hamilton Police on April 27, 2026.
The post-mortem examination determined that Marcin's death was the result of a homicide. The cause of death and details surrounding his injuries are not being released at this time in order to protect the integrity of the ongoing investigation.
Marcin's disappearance prompted an extensive missing person investigation. Investigators pursued numerous avenues in an effort to locate him after his absence was reported and quickly determined that his disappearance was out of character and inconsistent with his usual behaviour.
What began as a missing person investigation has now become a homicide investigation.
Hamilton Police extend condolences to Marcin's family and loved ones.
The Homicide Unit has assumed carriage of the investigation and is working to determine the circumstances surrounding Marcin's death and identify those responsible.
Investigators are asking anyone who had contact with Marcin, observed him, or has information regarding his activities and movements prior to his death to contact police. Investigators are also seeking information regarding Marcin's black 2014 Volkswagen Jetta with tinted windows.
Anyone with information is asked to contact Detective Tom Kersgens of the Homicide Unit at 905-546-4167. F
or those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website
.

title: A Bad Scent: Four Arrested in Hamilton Robbery Series
content:
download
Your browser does not support HTML5 video.
Shopper_Theft_MAY26
Hamilton Police have arrested four individuals following an investigation into a series of thefts and robberies targeting Shoppers Drug Mart locations across the city.
Beginning in April 2026, investigators examined multiple incidents involving the theft of high-value fragrance products from several Shoppers Drug Mart locations throughout Hamilton. Through surveillance review and investigative efforts, police identified suspects believed to be connected to a number of incidents.
As the investigation progressed, investigators observed an escalation in the incidents, with suspects using hammers to smash glass fragrance display cases before stealing large quantities of perfume and cologne products.
On May 21, 2026, at approximately 9:00 p.m., three male suspects attended the Shoppers Drug Mart located at 661 Upper James Street. One suspect used a hammer to smash fragrance display cabinets before the group fled with stolen merchandise in a grey Nissan Rogue. No physical injuries were reported.
On May 22, 2026, at approximately 1:49 a.m., police responded to a similar robbery at the Shoppers Drug Mart located at 510 Concession Street. Three male suspects entered the store armed with hammers, smashed display cases, stole fragrance products, and fled in the same vehicle.
Later that morning, at approximately 10:32 a.m., police were dispatched to a third robbery at the Shoppers Drug Mart located at 1000 Golf Links Road. During that incident, suspects aggressively smashed glass display cabinets while employees and customers fled the area in fear.
As part of the ongoing investigation, members of the Division 30 HEAT Unit located the suspect vehicle and arrested four individuals without incident.
Police seized:
• Two backpacks containing perfume and cologne products bearing Shoppers Drug Mart stickers
• One hammer
• One crowbar
• One knife
Investigators believe the seized items were used during the robberies.
The investigation is linked to 13 reported theft and robbery incidents across Hamilton dating back to April 2026, with reported losses exceeding $21,000.
The following accused have been charged:
Damian BENT-GALE, 21, Hamilton
• Robbery x3
• Theft Under $5,000 x9
Deshawn DAVIDSON, 27, Hamilton
• Robbery x3
• Theft Under $5,000 x2
• Fail to Comply with Probation
Brandon FERRIS, 35, Hamilton
• Robbery x3
Nicole MARTIN, 40, Hamilton
• Robbery x3
All accused parties were held for bail hearings.
Anyone with information related to these incidents is asked to contact the Hamilton Police Service Division 30 Criminal Investigations Branch at 905-546-3886.
For those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website
.

title: Hamilton Police Address Ongoing Disorder in East End Neighbourhood
content:
The Hamilton Police Service has laid multiple provincial charges following a joint enforcement initiative conducted by members of the Shooting Response Team and Division 10 Uniform Patrol at a licensed establishment in Hamilton’s east end.
The enforcement action was undertaken in response to ongoing community concerns and a pattern of incidents requiring repeated police response in the surrounding area.
The neighbourhood has been the subject of multiple police investigations, including:
A shooting in August 2025 in the immediate vicinity
A second shooting on April 18, 2026, which remains under investigation
During the course of those investigations, detectives became aware of alleged violations of liquor licence regulations and launched a separate compliance investigation.
Investigators also received ongoing complaints from residents and community members regarding:
Late-night disturbances
Suspected after-hours alcohol service
Disorder and safety concerns affecting the surrounding neighbourhood
On May 15, 2026, at approximately 4:00 a.m., officers with Division 10 Uniform Patrol and detectives with the Shooting Response Team attended Wally’s World Bar and Grill, located at 859 King Street East, to conduct a liquor licence inspection pursuant to the Liquor Licence and Control Act.
As a result of the inspection and subsequent investigation, officers laid charges related to:
The sale and service of alcohol outside permitted hours
Failure to comply with liquor licence conditions
Permitting intoxication and disorderly conduct
Smoking and cannabis use inside the establishment
Failure to maintain proper control of the premises
Obstructing police in the lawful execution of their duties
Charges have been laid against:
Wally World Bar & Grill Inc. (corporate licence holder)
An individual identified as the liquor licence holder
An individual identified as a bartender and owner of the corporation
Charges were laid under the:
Liquor Licence and Control Act and associated regulations
Smoke-Free Ontario Act
This investigation reflects the Hamilton Police Service’s continued response to ongoing community concerns involving disorder, safety, and non-compliance within the neighbourhood.
Hamilton Police recognize the impact that repeated incidents and ongoing disturbances have on residents, neighbouring businesses, and overall community well-being. Addressing locations linked to persistent calls for service and public safety concerns remains a priority.
Members of the public are encouraged to continue reporting concerns, as community information plays an important role in identifying and addressing problem locations.
To remain anonymous, contact Crime Stoppers of Hamilton at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit information online at crimestoppershamilton.com.

title: Can You Identify This Vehicle? Police Seek Assistance Following Collision
content:
Suspect Vehicle
Hamilton Police are continuing to investigate a motor vehicle collision that occurred on May 18, 2026, at approximately 7:30 p.m. in the area of Barton Street and McNeilly Road in Stoney Creek.
The collision involved an 8-year-old female pedestrian who was struck while riding her scooter. At the time of the incident, her injuries were believed to be serious; however, investigators have since confirmed that she sustained minor injuries.
Police are still attempting to identify the maroon pickup truck and trailer involved in the collision. A still image of the vehicle has been released in hopes that members of the public may recognize it.
Anyone with information that may assist investigators is asked to contact the on-duty Division 20 Staff Sergeant at 905-546-2963.
For those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website
.

title: Police Investigate Overnight Stabbing Near Rail Trail
content:
Hamilton Police are searching for a suspect following a stabbing incident that occurred in the area of Broadway Avenue and Ward Avenue.
On June 11, 2026, at approximately 1:00 a.m., Hamilton Police responded to reports of a stabbing in the area. Upon arrival, officers located a 29-year-old male suffering from multiple stab wounds. The victim was transported to a local hospital in life-threatening condition.
During the investigation, police learned that the suspect approached the victim on the sidewalk and began directing racial slurs toward him. The suspect then produced a knife and assaulted the victim before fleeing the area on a bicycle.
Officers, including members of the Canine Unit, conducted an extensive search of the surrounding area, including the nearby Rail Trail and golf course; however, the suspect was not located.
The suspect is described as:
Black male
Wearing a white shirt and hat
Last seen riding a bicycle eastbound toward the Rail Trail
Investigators have since learned that the victim's condition has improved and has been upgraded to stable.
Hamilton Police continue to investigate and are asking anyone with information, surveillance footage, or dash camera video from the area around the time of the incident to contact the on-duty Division 10 Staff Sergeant at 905-546-4725.
To provide information anonymously, contact Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477) or submit a tip online at crimestoppershamilton.com.

title: Hamilton Police Investigating Late Night Shooting
content:
download
Your browser does not support HTML5 video.
26662270 - Clip 1 - Media Redacted
Hamilton Police are investigating a late night shooting at a residence on Elodia Court in Hamilton. This is the second firearm-related incident in the area in the last four days.
On Sunday, June 7, 2026, at approximately 3:16 a.m., Hamilton Police responded to the area of Elodia Court after a witness reported a suspicious vehicle stopped in the neighbourhood. Officers arrived a short time later and located a grey Honda parked on Elodia Court with its lights off. Following a brief pursuit, the suspect was arrested and officers seized a loaded Glock handgun.
On Tuesday, June 9, 2026, at approximately 11:57 p.m., Hamilton Police received multiple 911 calls reporting shots fired at a residence on Elodia Court. When officers arrived, they located shell casings outside the residence and multiple bullet holes in the garage door.
A black Honda CR-V with licence plate DANH 172 was seen fleeing the area. The vehicle was reported stolen from the Peel region in May. Investigators are asking anyone with video or information that may assist the investigation to contact the Shooting Response Team at 905-546-4883 or emailing
[email protected]
.
There have also been three other incidents in the area targeted to the same address on Elodia Court. Police have remained in contact with the occupants of the targeted residence, who have been cooperative with investigators.
Hamilton Police will have a visible presence in the area as the investigation continues.
For those who wish to provide information anonymously, please consider reaching out to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477 or submit your anonymous tips online at
Crime Stoppers Hamilton's Website
.
Media Availability
: Staff Sergeant Steve Bereziuk will be available for media questions at 3:00 p.m. on Elodia Court.
